publicationstmt.publisher: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections

profiledesc.creation: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on 2022-09-19 18:40:00 UTC.

ead.archdesc:
======= RMM07744James Douglas Merritt papers7744Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections2B Carl A. Kroch LibraryCornell UniversityIthaca, NY 14853rareref@cornell.eduURL: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on 2022-09-19 19:04:46 UTC.appm >>>>>>> 2e3ec5c57314077b8435567632a49d313357daab
English
Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections
James Douglas Merritt papers
Merritt, James Douglas.
7744
9.0 cubic feet. 9.0 cubic feet.
1930-2002. <<<<<<< HEAD 4 ======= 4 >>>>>>> 2e3ec5c57314077b8435567632a49d313357daab 5958378
General Note
Collecting program: Human Sexuality Collection.
Scope and content
Personal journals, correspondence, and other personal papers.
Preferred Citation
James Douglas Merritt papers, #7744. Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections, Cornell University Library.
Biographical / Historical
Born Sept. 13, 1934, in Lebanon, N.H., he earned a doctorate in English at the University of Wisconsin-Madison in 1964 and served as an assistant professor at the University of Pittsburgh and then at Brooklyn College, where he became a full professor. He retired in 1995. Merritt and his partner, John Payne Hawker, bought a home in Provincetown, Mass. around 1970, where he spent most summers and made frequent trips in the winter. He died Dec. 23, 2004.
Gays' writings. Gays -- Social life and customs. Provincetown (Mass.) -- Social life and customs. Diaries.
5958378 <<<<<<< HEAD Journals and DatebooksJournals
Arrangement
Arranged chroniclogically
Processing Information
Journals removed from 3-ring binders since they were posing a preservation risk. Material that was in each binder was kept within the same folder. Notes on binders made by James were kept.Journals1965-10-21-1967-12-1811Journals1967-12-27-1969-11-3012Journals1970-01-12-1971-02-1213
